blocks.body: Reported Statements shows you how to report facts, opinions, and information after a reporting verb.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"Use reported statements when you want to pass on somebody else's opinion, message, or fact without direct quotation marks.","rules":[{"label":"Direct → reported","color":"emerald","text":"After say or tell, we build the statement into a new sentence.","example":"He said that he was tired."},{"label":"Backshift often happens","color":"blue","text":"If the reporting verb is in the past, the tense in the reported clause often moves one step back.","example":"I am busy. → She said that she was busy."},{"label":"Pronouns may change","color":"amber","text":"Together with the tense, pronouns, possessives, and situation details may also change.","example":"My brother is here. → He said that his brother was there."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. Form and structure
blocks.body:
The usual pattern is subject + said / told + (that) + reported clause.

  • say usually appears without a direct object: She said (that) she was late.
  • tell usually needs an object: She told me (that) she was late.
  • The linker that is often optional, especially in neutral speech.
  • The reported clause keeps normal statement word order because it is no longer a direct quote.

blocks.heading: 2. Main uses and examples
blocks.body:
Direct statementReported statementWhat changed
“I work from home.”She said that she worked from home.I → she, work → worked
“We have finished the task.”They said that they had finished the task.have finished → had finished
“My sister will call you.”He told me that his sister would call me.will → would, and the pronouns change too

We use these patterns when we retell news, opinions, explanations, or the result of a conversation.

blocks.heading: 3. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: keeping direct quotation inside the reported clause.
    Better: She said that she was tired, not She said that “I am tired”.
  • Mistake: forgetting to change the pronoun.
    Better: He said that he needed help if the speaker is he.
  • Mistake: treating backshift as automatic in every case. If the fact is still true, backshift may be optional: The teacher said that water boils at 100°C.

blocks.heading: 4. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• For statements, we most often use said (that) or told + object + (that).
  • After a past reporting verb, backshift is common.
  • Always check pronouns, possessives, and the context, not only the tense.
